title: Ceropegia muruganii
intro: Ceropegia muruganii, also known as the 'Tamil Rope Vine', is a succulent plant species native to India, characterized by its rope-like stems and beautiful white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Ceropegia muruganii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from the plant include extracts and infusions, often used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Ceropegia muruganii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to India and is often classified as a succulent due to its water-storing leaves. The species was first described in 2003, and its classification has been confirmed through various botanical studies. Ceropegia muruganii is closely related to other species within the Ceropegia genus, which shares similarities in leaf structure and flowering patterns. The plant's classification is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its twisted stems and funnel-shaped flowers.
